Course Title: Automation Bias in Pilots and Operators
Category: Transportation & Logistics: Aviation, Maritime, Logistics, Shipping, Automotive
Who is this for: Pilots, flight operators, air traffic controllers, ship captains, maritime officers, logistics coordinators, transport operators, fleet managers, aviation technicians, navigation specialists
Team or Dept this is for: Operations, Transportation, Logistics, Aviation, Maritime, Fleet Management, Safety
Description: Over-reliance on AI-assisted navigation and control systems.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with aviation or transport operations, either through professional experience or general interest, is all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key signs of automation bias in aviation and transport operations, and explain how over-reliance on AI-assisted navigation and control systems can compromise safety. You will also apply practical strategies to recognise and reduce automation bias in your day-to-day role as a pilot or operator.
